title: buddleja marrubiifolia var. utahensis
intro: Buddleja marrubiifolia var. utahensis is a lesser-known variety of the Buddleja genus, also known as a shrub or small tree with fragrant flowers, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The medicinal properties of Buddleja marrubiifolia var. utahensis are primarily attributed to its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and exhibit antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing. Buddleja marrubiifolia var. utahensis can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments.
